children.name: Prunella
children.description: Unassuming mouse-like birds that shuffle through dense undergrowth. Despite superficial similarity to sparrows the thin insect-eating bill reveals their true nature. The Dunnock has one of the most complex mating systems of any European bird — males and females both pursue multiple partners simultaneously, leading to intense sperm competition and cooperative chick-rearing.

children.name: Prunella modularis
children.description: An unobtrusive, slate-and-brown bird that shuffles mouse-like beneath hedges and feeders, easily overlooked despite being common. It has one of the most complex mating systems of any European bird, with both sexes taking multiple mates.

children.name: Prunella collaris
children.description: A robust accentor of high mountains from the Alps to Japan, breeding above the treeline on rocky slopes and descending to lower altitudes in winter; like the Dunnock it has a polygynandrous mating system.

children.name: Prunella montanella
children.description: A boldly patterned accentor with a buff supercilium that breeds in Siberian taiga; a famous vagrant, it appeared across Europe in an unprecedented influx in autumn 2016.

children.name: Prunella atrogularis
children.description: An accentor of the Ural and Central Asian mountains, named for its black throat and face framed by a pale eyebrow.

children.name: Prunella ocularis
children.description: A Middle Eastern accentor of arid montane scrub from Turkey to Iran, with a striking white eyebrow over a dark cheek.

children.name: Prunella fagani
children.description: A little-known accentor restricted to the highlands of southwestern Arabia, sometimes treated as a subspecies of Radde's Accentor.

children.name: Prunella koslowi
children.description: A pale, sandy accentor of the arid steppe and semi-desert of Mongolia and northern China, well camouflaged against bare ground.

children.name: Prunella rubeculoides
children.description: A Himalayan and Tibetan accentor with a rusty-orange breast band, breeding in high alpine meadows and often nesting near nomad encampments.

children.name: Prunella strophiata
children.description: An accentor of Himalayan scrub and forest edge, marked by a white-spotted eyebrow and a band of rufous across the breast.

children.name: Prunella immaculata
children.description: A handsome slate-grey accentor with a maroon back and pale eye, haunting the mossy undergrowth of high Himalayan and Chinese forests.

children.name: Prunella fulvescens
children.description: A pale brown accentor with a bold white eyebrow, widespread across the dry mountains and plateaus of Central Asia.

children.name: Prunella himalayana
children.description: A high-mountain accentor breeding from the Altai to the Himalayas, with rufous-streaked flanks and a spotted throat, gathering in flocks in winter.

children.name: Prunella rubida
children.description: A dark, richly coloured accentor endemic to Japan, breeding in mountain forests and conifer scrub on Hokkaido and Honshu.
